description: This map illustrates the geographical distribution of "Mapped Sites" (dark green polygons) within the "EEA-38 and the UK" (tan/beige landmass background). The legend indicates that the dark green areas represent "Mapped Sites", while the tan areas represent the geographical extent of the "EEA-38 and the UK". These mapped sites are identified as 4,790 Natura 2000 (N2K) sites, each including a 2km buffer zone, which together cover a total area of 631,820 km². The sites are broadly distributed across the European continent, with a notable density of smaller, fragmented areas in the Iberian Peninsula, France, Italy, Central Europe, and the Balkan region. Larger, but fewer, mapped sites are visible in Northern European countries like Sweden and Finland. A scale bar at the bottom right indicates distances of 0, 50, and 100 km.
